blockingIssues.description: `test-suite/src/test/java/TestSuiteSmokeTest.java` only asserts true and does not verify the `test-suite` dependency on `:core`, so the scaffold does not actually prove the module boundary claimed in the feature handoff.
`build.gradle` wires a handwritten placeholder `allureReport` task instead of real Allure Gradle integration, so report generation does not produce an actual Allure report.
`test-suite/build.gradle` writes a synthetic `sample-test-result.txt` file into `build/allure-results`, which is not Allure result data and makes the reporting path contract-mismatched.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that generated Gradle `build/` outputs should not be committed unless a task explicitly requires checked-in generated artifacts.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of `bootstrap-gradle-wrapper-scaffold` found committed `core/build/**` and `test-suite/build/**` artifacts in commit `7c66b402a9c49eed1579ef3ec59909c30aa64b9b`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen reporting instructions to require actual Allure Gradle/plugin integration and prohibit placeholder report/result generation when a feature fulfills Allure reporting assertions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of `bootstrap-allure-entrypoints` found a handwritten placeholder `allureReport` task and fake `sample-test-result.txt` artifacts instead of real Allure-compatible reporting in commit `e4005c12a47af1d00a0e393a992efa7846307835`.
